unit: PR7 — Delete root learnings/ (substrate-gap fork: griot capture has no raw-note import path)

contract.goal: Remove the 4 orphan .md files at root learnings/ and the learnings/ directory itself. The original PLAN.md PR7 wanted to 'migrate through griot capture pipeline' to eat own dog food, but discovered mid-execution that bin/griot lacks the verbs that path requires (no `init`, no `compact`, no `--from-note=<path>` for capture). User pivoted to option C (delete) from RESEARCH § 4. The 4 lessons (bulk-transforms wisdom, biome-version foot-gun, generator/antagonist pattern declaration, dependency-grep papercut) are good notes but have no live consumer today — nothing reads them, and the naming collides with `griot init`'s consumer-side gitignored `learnings/` (the gap RESEARCH § 4 surfaced).
acceptance_criteria: npm test passes (1133/1133 — no test touched learnings/ files).
node scripts/sync-shared.ts --check passes (learnings/ wasn't part of any sync direction; no drift impact).
bin/griot use --as=llm continues to return 'no rollup yet' (unchanged — there was never a rollup; the 4 files were inert).
rules_applied: User decision: 'just delete the 4 files' (forked from original PLAN PR7 when the substrate gap was surfaced).
RESEARCH § 4 option A: delete (lowest cost, lowest risk). The 4 lessons predate the capture pipeline; they were never wired up to anything.

Substrate-gap awareness: the 'eat own dog food' path requires extending bin/griot capture to accept raw notes (--from-note=<path>) or building a separate migration verb. That's a substrate-improvement project, queued as future work.
disqualifiers: Modifying any other files (PR7 is pure deletion).
Including the substrate gap fix (extending griot capture) in this PR — that's a separate substrate-improvement project.
Test failures or drift after the deletion.
inputs: projects/2026-05-21-repo-compartmentalize/PLAN.md (§ PR7 — original 'migrate through griot capture' approach)

actions: Surfaced the substrate gap mid-execution: bin/griot has no `init`/`compact` verbs, and `griot capture` requires --from-checkin or --evaluator-finding — no path for raw seed-note import.
Grilled user on the fork. Four options offered (move to seed-learnings, write migration script, delete, skip). User picked option C (delete).
Ran npm test → 1133/1133 passing (unchanged; nothing depended on learnings/ files).
Ran node scripts/sync-shared.ts --check → ok (4 plugins synced; learnings/ wasn't claimed by any sync direction).
Ran bin/griot use --as=llm → returns 'no rollup yet' (same as pre-PR7; nothing was generating a rollup).
Evaluator panel skipped — pure deletion, no logic. Verification rests on the diff (4 files + 1 dir gone) + tests pass + drift check + griot use baseline unchanged.
corrections: Mid-execution: PLAN.md PR7 cited `bin/griot init` and `bin/griot compact` verbs that don't exist in the actual griot CLI (verbs are use, capture, operator-checks, mediate-panel only). Also `griot capture` requires --from-checkin or --evaluator-finding — no raw-note import path. PLAN's optimism didn't match substrate reality. Forked to user-grill; user picked deletion (option C); PLAN's PR7 description in retrospect was aspirational. Captured in PR description + notes for follow-up substrate work.

changes_since_previous: PR6 (draft restructure) merged to main as #23. Phase-2 branch rebased onto updated main. PR7 closes out Phase 2 — Phase 3 (PRs 8-11) begins after PR7 merges.
notes_for_pr: Substrate-gap surfaced: bin/griot doesn't support raw seed-note import. The PLAN.md PR7 description assumed `griot init` + `griot capture --note` + `griot compact` verbs that don't exist. The original 'eat own dog food' framing requires substrate work (extend griot capture with --from-note=<path> OR add a dedicated seed-import verb). Captured for a future substrate-improvement project.

User picked deletion over migration. The 4 lessons (bulk-transforms wisdom, biome-version footgun, generator/antagonist pattern declaration, dependency-grep papercut) had no live consumer — nothing read them. The naming collision with `griot init`'s consumer-side gitignored `learnings/` (per RESEARCH § 4) is now also resolved by removing the colliding directory.

If you change your mind about preserving the content: the files are in git history at HEAD~7 or so (before PR1). A future PR could lift them into docs/seed-learnings/ (or anywhere else) without substrate work.
Evaluator panel skipped — pure deletion, no logic. Verification: 1133 tests pass; --check ok; griot use baseline unchanged.
